info.name: Nyaay Legal AI — Full API
info.description: Complete Postman collection for the Nyaay Legal AI backend (FastAPI, port 9000). Covers chat, session management, intake, complaint drafting, and document export. Run python app.py first.

variable.description: Backend base URL. Change to your server IP for remote testing.
Set automatically by the Send Message request via test script.
Set manually after intake/start returns a case_id.

request.description: Returns server status. Confirms the backend is up and the Anthropic API key is configured.
Returns ui.html as text/html. This is the frontend entry point — open in browser.

Send the first message. Pass session_id=null — the backend creates a new session and returns the ID. Save the returned session_id for all subsequent turns.

Key response fields:
- answer: AI attorney response text
- session_id: persist this across all turns
- offer_complaint: true = show Generate Complaint button
- case_type: detected case type (e.g. personal_injury)
- required_elements: list of legal elements needed for this case type

response.name: 200 — New session created

request.description: Continue an existing chat session. Always pass session_id from the first response. When offer_complaint becomes true, start the intake flow.
Demonstrates the NY jurisdiction filter. Non-NY questions are rejected with a polite message. offer_complaint=false, case_type='unsupported'.

response.name: 200 — Jurisdiction blocked

request.description: Returns all chat sessions ordered by creation date descending. Use to populate the sidebar session list.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Returns all messages for a session in chronological order. Use to restore chat history when a user reopens a previous session.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Deletes all messages in a session but keeps the session record itself.
Permanently deletes a session and all its messages. This is irreversible.

Call once when offer_complaint first becomes true in a /questions response.

Required fields:
- session_id: the chat session ID from /questions
- case_type: the detected case type (e.g. personal_injury)
- initial_text: the user's last message or a summary — used to auto-extract fields

The backend auto-fills every field it can detect from initial_text. The response shows what was auto-filled and what is still missing.

Save the returned case_id — it is used for all subsequent intake, draft, and document calls.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Call this in parallel with /questions for every user message after intake has started.

The backend extracts any new fields from the text, merges them with previously collected fields, re-validates, and returns the updated state.

Check is_complete=true to know all required fields are collected. When complete, the Generate Complaint button should be fully enabled.

response.name: 200 OK — fields updated

request.description: Returns the current intake state for a case. Use on page reload to restore the intake UI without re-running extraction.
Runs field-level + SOL validation and returns the result without changing any state. Use to check whether a case is ready to draft.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Marks the intake as force_draft=true. After this, POST /draft/{case_id} will proceed even if required fields are missing. Missing fields will appear as [UNKNOWN] in the generated complaint.

Show the attorney a warning listing which fields will be [UNKNOWN] before calling this.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Returns a 0–100 completion percentage and field breakdown. Poll this to drive a progress bar in the UI while the user is chatting.

Generates a formal NY legal complaint using Claude Sonnet. The draft is cached after the first call — subsequent calls return from_cache=true instantly with no additional API charge.

Returns 422 if required fields are missing and force_draft has not been set. Call PATCH /intake/{case_id}/force first to proceed with [UNKNOWN] placeholders.

word_count and unknown_count help the UI show quality indicators.

response.name: 200 OK — draft generated
response.body:
{
  "case_id": "c7d8e9f0-1234-5678-abcd-ef0123456789",
  "case_type": "personal_injury",
  "complaint": "SUPREME COURT OF THE STATE OF NEW YORK
COUNTY OF KINGS

MARIA GONZALEZ,
                Plaintiff,

    -against-

FASTFREIGHT INC. and JOHN DOE,
                Defendants.

Index No.: [UNKNOWN]

COMPLAINT
...",
  "from_cache": false,
  "word_count": 912,
  "unknown_count": 1
}

response.name: 422 — Missing required fields

request.description: Returns the cached complaint draft if one exists. Use on page reload to restore a previously generated draft. Returns 404 if no draft has been generated yet.

response.name: 200 OK — from cache
response.body:
{
  "case_id": "c7d8e9f0-1234-5678-abcd-ef0123456789",
  "case_type": "personal_injury",
  "complaint": "SUPREME COURT OF THE STATE OF NEW YORK...",
  "from_cache": true,
  "word_count": 912,
  "unknown_count": 1
}

response.name: 404 — No draft yet

request.description:
Generates and downloads the complaint as a Word (.docx) file. The attorney_name field is injected into the signature block. If omitted, [ATTORNEY NAME] appears as a placeholder.

Requires POST /draft/{case_id} to have been called first.

In the browser use a fetch() with blob() to trigger the download.

response.name: 422 — No draft yet

request.description: Same as Download DOCX but returns a PDF. Uses ReportLab (pure Python — no Node.js). Requires POST /draft/{case_id} first.
Checks whether a draft exists and is ready to export. Does not generate any file. Use to decide whether to enable download buttons.
